children.heading: Special Handling of Trade Secret or Confidential Information.—
children.label: b Special Handling of Trade Secret or Confidential Information.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator, for a period of up to 5 years after the development of information described in paragraph (2), may provide appropriate protections against the dissemination of such information, including exemption from subchapter II of chapter 5 of title 5.

children.heading: .—Information described
children.label: 2 .—Information described
children.text: Information referred to in paragraph (1) is information that results from activities conducted under an agreement entered into under subsections (e) and (f) of , and that would be a trade secret or commercial or financial information that is privileged or confidential under the meaning of if the information had been obtained from a non-Federal party participating in such an agreement.section 20113 of this titlesection 552(b)(4) of title 5

children.heading: .—Committees of Congress
children.label: c .—Committees of Congress
children.text: Nothing in this chapter authorizes the withholding of information by the Administrator from the duly authorized committees of Congress.
